intro: Unit conversions are essential in science, engineering, and everyday life. The metric system (SI) is the global standard; the US and some territories use imperial units. This speed converter lets you instantly convert between km/h, mph, knots, and m/s. Fast, free, and updated for 2026.
keyTakeaway: **Formula: 1 km/h = 0.621 mph = 0.54 knots**. Enter any speed value and select the unit to convert instantly across all common velocity measurements.
useCases: Quick reference for automotive and aviation speed conversions
Students and professionals learning or applying unit conversions
International travel planning and vehicle specifications
explanation:
**Unit conversions** are fundamental to science, engineering, and everyday life. The metric system (SI) is the global standard; the US and some territories maintain imperial units.

## How It Works

This speed converter uses standard international conversion formulas to transform between km/h, mph, knots, and m/s. Enter any value and select its unit—the calculator will instantly display the equivalent in all other common speed units.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference purposes. For critical decisions (vehicles, aircraft, maritime operations), always verify with official specifications or consult a professional. Conversion factors are current as of 2026 and based on internationally standardized values.

faq.q: How do I convert km/h to mph?
faq.a: Multiply the km/h value by 0.621. For example, 100 km/h × 0.621 = 62.1 mph.
A knot is one nautical mile per hour, used in maritime and aviation. 1 knot = 1.852 km/h = 1.151 mph.

faq.q: How fast is 100 km/h in mph?

faq.a: Meters per second (m/s) is standard in physics, meteorology, and wind speed measurements.

faq.q: Why do different countries use different speed units?
faq.a: The US uses mph due to historical reasons, while most countries adopted the metric system. Maritime transport uses knots globally.
Yes, this calculator is completely free with no registration or sign-up required.
These use standard international conversion formulas validated by NIST and other agencies. For critical applications, always verify with official sources.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't transmit or store any data on external servers.
